summary: Surah An-Nazi‘at swears by angels who wrench out the souls of deniers and gently release the believers, moving swiftly to execute Allah’s command. It depicts the quake of the Hour and the single Cry after which all will be on a plain. The story of Musa and Pharaoh shows the fate of arrogance. Allah’s power is evidenced by the structuring of the heavens, spreading of the earth, waters, pasture, and mountains—for your benefit. When the Greatest Catastrophe arrives, Hell is for those who rebel and prefer worldly life; Paradise is for those who fear standing before their Lord and restrain desires. The timing of the Hour belongs to Allah alone; when they see it, their worldly stay will seem but an evening or morning.
